channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Iran fires at least 180 missiles into Israel as regionwide conflict grows
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Iran launched at least 180 missiles into Israel on Tuesday, the latest in a series of rapidly escalating attacks between Israel and Iran and its Arab allies that threatens to push the Middle East closer to a regionwide war.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, AAMER MADHANI, and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Kentucky sheriff seen in footage shooting at judge in shocking preliminary hearing
item.description: Video shown at the preliminary hearing for a former Kentucky sheriff, accused of killing a district judge, allegedly showed the deadly shooting.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CBS cuts mics of Vance, Walz during immigration argument
item.description: The sparring started when Brennan clarified for debate viewers that Springfield “does have a large number of Haitian migrants who have legal status, temporary protected status.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Walz on Tiananmen Square screw-up: 'I'm a knucklehead at times'
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D), pressed at the vice presidential debate over false statements he made in which he said he was present during the Tiananmen Square protests in 1989, said: “I’m a knucklehead at times.
item.dc:creator: Ian Swanson, KREX 5

item.title: CBS cuts mics of Vance, Walz during immigration argument
item.description: The sparring started when Brennan clarified for debate viewers that Springfield “does have a large number of Haitian migrants who have legal status, temporary protected status.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KREX 5

item.title: Trump rips Walz, CBS moderators in debate's opening 15 minutes
item.description: “Walz is taking so many notes - Never seen a Candidate take more! He needs the notes to keep his brain intact,” Trump wrote.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Walz misleadingly claims to have been in Hong Kong during period tied to Tiananmen Square massacre
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Multiple news reports indicate that Democratic vice presidential nominee Minnesota Gov.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and DIDI TANG Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Search crews with cadaver dogs wade through muck of communities ‘wiped off the map’ by Helene
item.description: SWANNANOA, N.C. (AP) — Cadaver dogs and search crews trudged through knee-deep muck and debris in the mountains of western North Carolina on Tuesday looking for more victims of Hurricane Helene days after the storm carved a deadly and destructive path through the Southeast.
item.dc:creator: ERIK VERDUZCO, JEFF AMY and KIMBERLEE KRUESI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about Iran's missile barrage and Israel's ground operations in Lebanon
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Iran launched at least 180 missiles at Israel on Tuesday evening, causing scattered damage and fires from falling shrapnel, but Israeli authorities said there were no injuries. An Israeli security official said most of the missiles were intercepted, though some managed to land.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A Texas man is executed for fatally stabbing twin teenage girls in 1989
item.description: HUNTSVILLE, Texas (AP) — A Texas man convicted of fatally stabbing twin 16-year-old girls more than three decades ago was executed on Tuesday evening. Garcia Glenn White was pronounced dead at 6:56 p.m. CDT following a chemical injection at the state penitentiary in Huntsville.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO and MICHAEL GRACZYK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: School board to pay $575K to teacher fired for not using transgender student's pronouns
item.description: A Virginia school board has agreed to pay a former high school teacher more than $500,000 in damages and attorneys' fees as part of a legal settlement after the instructor's dismissal in 2018 for refusing to use a transgender student's pronouns.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Massachusetts dealership scheme victim: 'I feel like I'm living in a movie'
item.description: Edward Cicciu, 46, of Attleboro, is accused of receiving cars on loan from local auctions but not obtaining the titles.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Brill, NewsNation

item.title: NYC mayor deflects questions about bribery charges as a potential witness speaks outside City Hall
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams delivered his most thorough public defense to date since his indictment on federal bribery charges, insisting the case was a “test” that would not distract him from governing as he deflected specific questions about the investigation for more than an ho
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Election forecaster shifts Bacon district in Nebraska toward Democrat
item.description: The election forecaster Sabato’s Crystal Ball announced Monday it shifted Rep. Don Bacon’s (R-Neb.) race in Nebraska toward the Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Judge rejects computer repairman's defamation claims over reports on Hunter Biden laptop
item.description: DOVER, Del. (AP) — A computer repairman at the center of a controversy over Hunter Biden's laptop has lost his defamation case against news outlets, the president's son and Joe Biden's presidential campaign.
item.dc:creator: RANDALL CHASE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Sean 'Diddy' Combs accused of sexual misconduct by 120 people, attorney says
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — An attorney said Tuesday he is representing 120 accusers who have come forward with sexual misconduct allegations against Sean “Diddy” Combs, the hip-hop mogul who is awaiting trial on sex trafficking charges.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump takes lead over Harris in North Carolina Quinnipiac survey
item.description: Trump leads with 49 percent support, Harris earns 47 percent support, Green Party candidate Jill Stein gets 1 percent support and Justice For All Party candidate Cornel West gets 1 percent support in the Quinnipiac University poll conducted in late September and released Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Speaker Johnson says Congress has provided funds for Helene response
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) on Tuesday said Congress has already authorized the disaster relief funds needed for the immediate response to Helene, the storm that has ravaged multiple states from Florida up through western North Carolina and Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: McDonald’s is bringing back its famous ‘Boo Buckets,’ with a new color
item.description: McDonald's famous Boo Buckets are making a return just in time for Halloween, and a new color has joined the lineup this year.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Dockworkers may have the negotiating advantage in their strike against US ports
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — The 45,000 dockworkers who went on strike Tuesday for the first time in decades at 36 U.S. ports from Maine to Texas may wield the upper hand in their standoff with port operators over wages and the use of automation.
item.dc:creator: TOM KRISHER, WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS and TASSANEE VEJPONGSA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wall Street retreats from records and oil prices jump after Iran's missile attack on Israel
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. stocks retreated from their records Tuesday after Iran fired missiles into Israel, a sharp escalation of tensions in the Middle East that investors fear could lead to disruptions in the flow of oil.
item.dc:creator: STAN CHOE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Students say they're fed up with Ohio high school bathroom policy
item.description: "Today, I had to use the bathroom so bad and my teacher gave me permission to go run there real quick and all of them were locked," Freshman Nora Hardesty said.
item.dc:creator: Katie Millard, NewsNation

item.title: JD Vance, Tim Walz face off in 2024 VP debate: Key takeaways
item.description: Republican J.D. Vance and Democrat Tim Walz met Tuesday in the lone vice presidential debate of the 2024 election, bringing together undercards who have spent two months going after each other and the opposing nominees who top the major-party tickets.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KTLA 5

item.title: Some Carter Co. communities 'completely cut off'
item.description: CARTER COUNTY, Tenn. (WJHL) — Parts of Carter County are still without communication as several people remain missing after historic flooding hit the region. On Monday morning, Carter County leaders held a press conference to provide updates on the county's status after the floods.
item.dc:creator: Anslee Daniel, NewsNation

item.title: Hair found in truck helps police identify suspect in 1995 disappearance of Morgan Nick, 6
item.description: ALMA, Ark. (AP) — Hair found in a truck once owned by an Arkansas man who died in prison more than two decades ago was the clue investigators needed to connect the dead inmate to the disappearance of a 6-year-old girl who went missing in 1995.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DEMILLO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The Latest: Kirk becomes a hurricane as North Carolina reels from Helene
item.description: Desperate residents of the storm-battered mountains of western North Carolina lined up for water and food, hunted for cellphone signals and slogged buckets from creeks to flush toilets days after Hurricane Helene’s remnants deluged the region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Helene's death toll surpasses 150 as crews continue to search for survivors
item.description: Exhausted emergency workers continued to work around-the-clock to clear roads, restore power and cellphone service, and reach people left stranded by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Helene and other storms dumped a whopping 40 trillion gallons of rain on the South
item.description: More than 40 trillion gallons of rain drenched the Southeast United States in the last week from Hurricane Helene and a run-of-the-mill rainstorm that sloshed in ahead of it — an unheard of amount of water that has stunned experts.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump suggests ‘1 rough hour’ of policing would end retail crime
item.description: The former president called for law enforcement to be freed up to "do their job" without restrictions.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Salt Life is closing all stores across 10 states: See the full list of locations
item.description: Salt Life, a brand synonymous with Florida’s ocean-loving culture, is closing all of its physical stores as part of a significant transition to a wholesale and e-commerce business model.
item.dc:creator: Brody Wooddell, KRON4

item.title: 3 ex-officers did not testify at their trial in Tyre Nichols' death
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Lawyers for three former Memphis officers rested their cases Monday without calling them to testify in their own defense against charges that they violated Tyre Nichols’ civil rights in a beating that proved fatal following a 2023 traffic stop.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: North Carolina official reveals shocking damage to tourist town: 'All of it was washed into the lake'
item.description: One North Carolina GOP national committeeman, whose family has lived on Lake Lure for generations, recounts how the tourist town of Chimney Rock has been devastated.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden administration expands asylum restrictions at southern border
item.description: The Biden administration announced Monday it will expand its asylum restrictions at the U.S.-Mexico border, cementing tougher standards that will cut off access to protections for many migrants.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Basketball Hall of Famer Dikembe Mutombo dies at 58
item.description: Mutombo starred in the NBA for 18 seasons and is considered to be one of the greatest defensive players in the history of the sport.
item.dc:creator: Brayden Stamps, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Stuck NASA astronauts welcome SpaceX capsule that'll bring them home next year
item.description: The two astronauts stuck at the International Space Station since June welcomed their new ride home with Sunday’s arrival of a SpaceX capsule.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: When do the Jewish High Holidays start? The 10-day season begins this week with Rosh Hashana
item.description: Rosh Hashana, the Jewish new year, starts at sundown on Wednesday, Oct. 2, and continues through the evening of Friday, Oct. 4. It marks the start of the Jewish High Holidays, a 10-day season that ends with Yom Kippur, the Day of Atonement.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4
